stimulus:
Text 1

One difficulty faced by researchers comparing rates of homelessness across countries is that nations may define homelessness differently. Some governments count only individuals sleeping in streets or shelters; others include people living temporarily with friends, in cars, or in informal settlements. Sweden, for example, uses four broad categories spanning short-term and long-term housing instability, while several other countries register only those who appear on shelter rolls. This variation makes cross-national comparisons of homelessness particularly difficult.

 

Text 2

Recently, a working group convened by several international housing agencies issued a unified framework for measuring homelessness across countries. The framework, known as the “global homelessness typology,” specifies common categories—rough sleeping, emergency accommodation, and severely insecure housing—and provides explicit criteria for placing individuals within them, enabling researchers to make more meaningful international comparisons.
stem: Based on the texts, how would the author of Text 2 most likely respond to the problem presented in Text 1?

answerOptions.content: By recommending that a specific country should lead further investigation into the problem
By proposing that researchers focus on social issues besides homelessness
By indicating that a possible remedy for the problem is already in place
By describing additional ways in which homelessness research is difficult

rationale:
Choice C is the best answer. Text 1 identifies a difficulty in homelessness research: countries define homelessness differently, frustrating cross-national comparison. Text 2 describes the “global homelessness typology,” a unified framework with common categories and explicit criteria that lets researchers make more meaningful international comparisons. The author of Text 2 would respond by noting that a possible remedy for the problem of inconsistent definitions is already available.

Choice A is incorrect because Text 2 attributes the framework to a working group of agencies rather than singling out a country to lead further research. Choice B is incorrect because both texts concern homelessness; neither proposes redirecting attention to other issues. Choice D is incorrect because Text 2 offers a solution to the definitional problem rather than adding further difficulties.
